item.text: I’ll locate every direct `run_deterministic(...)` call via Orbit graph shell commands, then verify each call site’s containing `fn` before listing the exact path/function pairs. I’m starting by checking the available `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` command surface.

item.aggregated_output:
Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

Usage: orbit tool [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list      List all registered tools
  show      Show detailed information about a tool
  run       Execute a tool
  add       Register an external tool or MCP plugin
  scaffold  Generate a starter external tool plugin
  remove    Remove an external tool
  enable    Enable a disabled tool
  disable   Disable a tool
  doctor    Validate tool health

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

Examples:
  orbit tool scaffold ./plugins/hello_orbit.py --name demo.hello
  orbit tool add ./plugins/hello_orbit.py
  orbit tool show demo.hello

item.text: I confirmed `orbit tool run` usage; next I’m enumerating registered tools so we can use only the `orbit.graph.*` ones for evidence.

item.aggregated_output:
error: unexpected argument '--output' found

Usage: orbit tool list [OPTIONS]

For more information, try '--help'.

List all registered tools

Usage: orbit tool list [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --json         Output as JSON
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help
error: store error: attempt to write a readonly database

item.text: I hit a tooling constraint: `orbit tool show orbit.graph.search` failed with `attempt to write a readonly database`. I’m now probing whether graph calls can run in a purely read-only mode or with an alternate writable temp state path so we can stay within the benchmark constraints.

    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs#V2DispatchInput:struct",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs#DispatchOutcome:struct"
  ],
  "source": "fn run_deterministic(\n    host: &dyn V2RuntimeHost,\n    spec: &DeterministicSpec,\n    fs_profile: Option<&str>,\n    audit: Arc<V2AuditWriter>,\n    input: &Value,\n) -> Result<DispatchOutcome, DispatchError> {\n    let tool_context =\n        host.tool_context_for_activity(fs_profile, Some(v2_fs_audit_logger(audit.clone())));\n    let output = host.run_deterministic(&spec.action, &spec.config, input, tool_context)?;\n    Ok(DispatchOutcome {\n        success: true,\n        output,\n        message: None,\n    })\n}"
}

/bin/zsh -lc "python -c 'import json,subprocess;root=\"/Users/daniel/workspace/repos/orbit\";run=lambda c: subprocess.check_output(c,cwd=root,text=True);refs=json.loads(run([\"orbit\",\"tool\",\"run\",\"orbit.graph.refs\",\"--input\",\"{\\\"selector\\\":\\\"symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs#V2RuntimeHost:trait\\\",\\\"include\\\":[\\\"code\\\"]}\"]))[\"code_refs\"];sels=[]
for r in refs:
    if r[\"file\"].startswith(\"crates/orbit-engine/src/\") and r[\"kind\"] in (\"function\",\"method\"):
        sels.append(r[\"selector\"])

for sel in sels:
    if sel in seen: continue
    seen.add(sel)
    data=json.loads(run([\"orbit\",\"tool\",\"run\",\"orbit.graph.show\",\"--input\",json.dumps({\"selector\":sel,\"include\":{\"source\":True}})]))
    src=data.get(\"source\") or \"\"
    print((\"MATCH\" if \".run_deterministic(\" in src else \"NO\")+\"\\t\"+data[\"selector\"])'"

    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/agent_loop_driver.rs#drive_agent_loop_with_session:function",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/agent_loop_driver.rs#drive_agent_loop_with_tool_context:function"
  ],
  "source": "pub fn drive_agent_loop(\n    spec: &AgentLoopSpec,\n    api_key: Option<&str>,\n    run_id: &str,\n    audit: Arc<V2AuditWriter>,\n    input: &Value,\n    host: &dyn V2RuntimeHost,\n    fs_profile: Option<&str>,\n) -> Result<LoopOutcome, DispatchError> {\n    let model = resolve_model(spec);\n    let provider = expected_provider();\n    let mut session = Session::new(provider, model.clone(), &spec.instruction, None);\n    let tool_ctx =\n        host.tool_context_for_activity(fs_profile, Some(v2_fs_audit_logger(audit.clone())));\n    drive_inner(spec, api_key, run_id, audit, &mut session, input, tool_ctx)\n}"
}

  "source": "pub fn drive_agent_loop_with_session(\n    spec: &AgentLoopSpec,\n    api_key: Option<&str>,\n    run_id: &str,\n    audit: Arc<V2AuditWriter>,\n    session: &mut Session,\n    input: &Value,\n    host: &dyn V2RuntimeHost,\n    fs_profile: Option<&str>,\n) -> Result<LoopOutcome, DispatchError> {\n    let tool_ctx =\n        host.tool_context_for_activity(fs_profile, Some(v2_fs_audit_logger(audit.clone())));\n    drive_inner(spec, api_key, run_id, audit, session, input, tool_ctx)\n}"
}

  "siblings": [
    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs#V2RuntimeHost:trait",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs#V2DispatchInput:struct",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/dispatcher.rs#DispatchOutcome:struct"
  ],
  "source": "fn run_deterministic(\n    host: &dyn V2RuntimeHost,\n    spec: &DeterministicSpec,\n    fs_profile: Option<&str>,\n    audit: Arc<V2AuditWriter>,\n    input: &Value,\n) -> Result<DispatchOutcome, DispatchError> {\n    let tool_context =\n        host.tool_context_for_activity(fs_profile, Some(v2_fs_audit_logger(audit.clone())));\n    let output = host.run_deterministic(&spec.action, &spec.config, input, tool_context)?;\n    Ok(DispatchOutcome {\n        success: true,\n        output,\n        message: None,\n    })\n}"
}

  "selector": "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/groundhog.rs#load_state:function",
  "siblings": [
    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/groundhog.rs#CHRONICLE_ARTIFACT_PATH:field",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/groundhog.rs#STATE_ARTIFACT_PATH:field",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/groundhog.rs#DEFAULT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS:field"
  ],
  "source": "fn load_state(\n    host: &dyn V2RuntimeHost,\n    tool_ctx: &ToolContext,\n    task_id: &str,\n) -> Result<GroundhogRunnerState, DispatchError> {\n    let artifacts = load_task_artifacts(host, tool_ctx, task_id)?;\n    let Some(artifact) = artifacts\n        .into_iter()\n        .find(|artifact| artifact.path == STATE_ARTIFACT_PATH)\n    else {\n        return Ok(GroundhogRunnerState::default());\n    };\n\n    serde_json::from_str(&artifact.content).map_err(|error| {\n        DispatchError::GroundhogFailed(format!(\n            \"parse runner state artifact for task `{task_id}`: {error}\"\n        ))\n    })\n}"
}
